title: Claude Cowork - From delegation to deliverables
content:
## From delegation to deliverables
Spend less time finding, formatting, and fixing.

## Filling a gap for knowledge workers
Chat is a useful resource in many instances, but it's not always the right tool for your needs. At Anthropic, non-technical teams like Marketing and Data started bypassing Claude's chat interface for Claude Code, drawn to its ability to handle complex, multi-step work like building tools or mining data. We observed the same pattern externally. Claude Cowork is the result: the same capability with a simplified experience, designed for where non-technical knowledge work happens.

## Built for non-technical tasks
Most AI assistants require users to break work into individual prompts. Claude Cowork takes the outcome and handles the rest.

Claude Cowork runs on desktop, where most knowledge work is done: in local files, folders, and the applications people use every day. It moves between them, synthesizes information across multiple sources, and completes tasks without the user coordinating each step. The work it handles best is high-effort and repeatable. No technical background is required.

In our research preview, work gets done faster. We also found that tedious tasks that might otherwise get skipped–like scanning data or feedback–now get done, leading to better decisions.

## Common workflows and use cases

### Organizing and managing local files
File systems accumulate faster than anyone can organize them. Point Claude at a folder of drafts, downloads, and attachments and ask it to rename, sort, deduplicate, or surface what's relevant.

### Preparing documents from source files
The hardest part of writing a report is rarely the writing. Hand off a set of source files and it produces a structured draft, handling assembly and synthesis so the work that's left is refinement.

### Synthesizing complex research
Reading across sources takes time that most people don't have. Share a question and a set of sources, and Claude identifies what's relevant and returns a summary ready for review.

### Extracting data from unstructured files
Contracts, reports, and records are dense by nature. Claude reads through them and returns the information that matters in a clear, digestible, structured format.
